records.ety: [L. declamare; de- + clamare to cry out: cf. F. dclamer. See Claim.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Declaimed (d*klāmd"); p. pr. & vb. n. Declaiming.]

defs: To speak rhetorically; to make a formal speech or oration; to harangue; specifically, to recite a speech, poem, etc., in public as a rhetorical exercise; to practice public speaking; as, the students declaim twice a week.
To speak for rhetorical display; to speak pompously, noisily, or theatrically; to make an empty speech; to rehearse trite arguments in debate; to rant.
quotes: Grenville seized the opportunity to declaim on the repeal of the stamp act.

defs: To utter in public; to deliver in a rhetorical or set manner.
To defend by declamation; to advocate loudly.
